title: An Objective Diagnosis Model with Integrated Metabolic and Immunity Parameters for Phlegm-Dampness Constitution
abstract: Background. According to Chinese constitutional theory, people can be divided into nine constitutions, which represent distinctive vulnerability to different diseases such as metabolic syndrome, atherosclerosis, and immunity-related disease, and so forth in modern medicine, phlegm-dampness constitution (PDC) is one of the nine constitutions, which is susceptible to metabolic syndrome (MS) and atherosclerosis that associate with lipid metabolism and immunity dysregulation closely. Objectives. In this study, we aimed to investigate the metabolic and immunity profiles of phlegm-damp constitution (PDC), including metabolites, lymphocytes distribution, and inflammatory cytokines. Methods. A total of 74 patients with PDC and 66 individuals with gentle constitution (GC) were enrolled in this study. We utilized biochemical methods to detect metabolic parameters, flow cytometry to survey T/B/NK/NKT lymphocyte subgroups distribution, and ELISA to assay inflammatory cytokines. Results. The subjects with PDC had higher GLU, AI TC, TG, and LDL-C and lower HDL-C levels. The immunity profile indicated that PDC subjects had higher percentage of WBCs, neutrophils, lymphocytes, B cells, and natural killer T cells compared with subjects with GC ( P < 0.05 ). Serum levels of IL-10 decreased significantly in the subjects with phlegm-damp constitution, whereas IL-12 levels increased dramatically in the PDC group compared with the GC group (both P < 0.05 ). Additionally, logistic regression identified four independent variables (GLU, TG, LDL-C, and lymphocytes) that were highly correlated with PDC ( P < 0.05 ). The area under the curve of the receiver operating characteristic curve was 0.878, which indicated the data were reliable to distinguish the subjects with PDC from the ones with GC. Conclusion. Phlegm-damp constitution was prone to hyperglycemia and hyperlipidemia syndrome, promoting the occurrence and progression of metabolic-related diseases. Interestingly, proinflammatory cells and cytokines were activated in the PDC group as well. Our findings could offer a profile of early screening indicators to identify high-risk patients of metabolic- and immunity-related diseases from Chinese constitution.

title: Analysis of influencing factors for predisposition to metabolic syndrome in adult women with phlegm-dampness constitution
abstract: Objective:To approach the major risk factors for metabolic syndrome (MS) in adult women with phlegmdampness constitution,so as to provide evidence for the prevention and treatment of MS in women.Methods:A total of 439 adult female volunteers with phlegm-dampness constitution were selected by a matched case-control study for questionnaire survey and physical examination,and the derived data was subject to univariate and multivariate Logistic regression analysis of the risk factors correlated to MS.Results:The factors including age,BMI,hypertension,waist circumference,hip circumference,waist-hip ratio,body fat percentage,fasting blood glucose,fasting insulin,insulin resistance,triglyceride,family history of diabetes,family history of hypertension,meat-based diet,stress,sleeping habit and phlegm-dampness constitution were statistically significant for the differences in MS among adult women with phlegm-dampness constitution (P＜0.05).Multivariate Logistic regression analysis identified age (OR=1.047),hypertension (OR=13.720),waist-hip ratio (OR=3.122),body fat percentage (OR=1.097),family history of hypertension (OR=2.407),and sleeping habit (OR=1.476) as the major risk factors for MS among adult women with phlegm-dampness constitution.Conclusion:The metabolic syndrome among adult women with phlegm-dampness constitution is related to age,hypertension,waist-hip ratio,body fat percentage,family history of hypertension,and bad sleeping habit.Early screening in the phlegm-dampness constitution to find out high-risk factors is an important measure for MS prevention and control.It is expectable to improve phlegm-dampness constitution,prevent the disease and promote the health by changing the convertible factors like lifestyles and behaviors.

title: Application of Artificial Intelligence in the Establishment of an Association Model between Metabolic Syndrome, TCM Constitution, and the Guidance of Medicated Diet Care
abstract: Background. This study conducted exploratory research using artificial intelligence methods. The main purpose of this study is to establish an association model between metabolic syndrome and the TCM (traditional Chinese medicine) constitution using the characteristics of individual physical examination data and to provide guidance for medicated diet care. Methods. Basic demographic and laboratory data were collected from a regional hospital health examination database in northern Taiwan, and artificial intelligence algorithms, such as logistic regression, Bayesian network, and decision tree, were used to analyze and construct the association model between metabolic syndrome and the TCM constitution. Findings. It was found that the phlegm-dampness constitution (90.6%) accounts for the majority of TCM constitution classifications with a high risk of metabolic syndrome, and high cholesterol, blood glucose, and waist circumference were statistically significantly correlated with the phlegm-dampness constitution. This study also found that the age of patients with metabolic syndrome has been advanced, and shift work is one of the risk indicators. Therefore, based on the association model between metabolic syndrome and TCM constitution, in the future, metabolic syndrome can be predicted through the syndrome differentiation of the TCM constitution, and relevant medicated diet care schemes can be recommended for improvement. Conclusion. In order to increase the public’s knowledge and methods for mitigating metabolic syndrome, in the future, nursing staff can provide nonprescription medicated diet-related nursing guidance information via the prediction and assessment of the TCM constitution.

title: Comparison of serum NO and related indexes of obesity:phlegm-dampness constitution, non phlegm-dampness constitution and metabolic syndrome
abstract: Objective To investigate the serum concentration of NO and related indexes of obesity with Phlegm-dampness constitution(OPD),compared with obesity with no Phlegm-dampness constitution(ONPD)and Metabolic Syn-drome(MS),so as to reveal the endothelial function of OPD,which can provide guidance for taking precautions against MS and Cardio-cerebrovascular Complications from the perspective of Phlegm-dampness constitution. Methods Four groups of volunteers were chose in this study:33 cases of obesity with Phlegm-dampness constitution(OPD),31 cases of obesity with NO Phlegm-dampness constitution(ONPD),24 cases of metabolic syndrome group(MS),and 25cases of the normal balanced constitution group(N). In each group,we detect obesity related indices such as BMI,WC,F% ,and MS related indices such as DBP,SBP,FPG,TG,HDL-C,LDL-C and TC. We detect NO related indices such as NOS,iNOS, insulin and ralculated the Insulin Resistance Indexes HOMA-IR. Results Compared with the group N,obesity and MS related indices were significantly higher or lower in the group MS(P < 0. 05 or P < 0. 01). In the group ONPD,there were no significant differences in other indices(P >0. 05). In the group OPD,Obesity related indices were significantly higher(P <0. 01),SBP,DBP,TG were significantly higher(P < 0. 05),HDL-C,and HDL-C were significantly lower (P <0. 01),but there were no significant differences with FPG,LDL-C,TC. In group OPD ,Obesity related indices were similar(P >0. 05),and DBP,TG were significantly higher,and other MS indices were similar(P >0. 05)than the group ONPD. As for NO,compared with N,there were no significant differences in ONPD ,but significantly higher in OPD and MS(P <0. 05 or P <0. 01). As for NOS,compared with N,there were no significant differences in ONPD and MS ,but significantly higher in OPD(P <0. 05). As for iNOS,compared with N,there were no significant differences in ONPD , but significantly higher in OPD and MS(P < 0. 05). There were also significant differences between OPD and ONPD (P <0. 05). As for IR,compared with N,there were no statistical significance in ONPD(P >0. 05). Compared with ON-PD,there were also significant differences in OPD and MS(P < 0. 05). Conclusion The persons with OPD have had vascular endothelial dysfunction even not reaching the clinical diagnostic criteria of MS,with the significant differences of NO,iNOS,IR. Obesity whether with Phlegm-dampness constitution is the key risk factor for endothelial dysfunction. So,we should pay close attention to the endothelial function of persons with Phlegm-dampness constitution and give early intervention and treatment,taking advantage of Traditional Chinese Medicine“Preventive Treatment of Disease”,which has important significance for preventing MS and cardio-cerebrovascular complications.

title: Study on related risk factors of metabolic syndrome among people with phlegm-dampness constitution
abstract: Objective:To explore the related risk factors of metabolic syndrome (MS) among people with phlegmdampness constitution and provide the basis for the prevention and control measures.Methods:With the case-controlled study design,730 people with phlegm-dampness constitution between 18 and 65 years old were selected.The data collected from clinic was used to analyze the risk factors for MS by Logistic regression analysis.Results:Among the common risk factors,hypertension (OR=25.04,95％CI[12.80,49.00]),high triglyceride (TG) (OR=3.40,95％CI[2.52,4.58]),high fasting plasma glucose (FPG)(OR=2.34,95％CI[1.79,3.05]),family history of hypertension (OR=2.00,95％CI[1.15,3.48]),big waist circumference (OR=1.06,95％CI[1.03,1.10]),high percentage of body fat (OR=1.03,95％CI[1.00,1.05]),low high-density lipoprotein cholesterol (HDL-C)(OR=0.02,95％CI[0.00,0.05]) were greater risk factors for MS.Conclusion:There were correlations between MS among the people with phlegm-dampness constitution and hypertension,TG,FPG,family history of hypertension,waist circumference,percentage of body fat,HDL-C,which should be exploited in the process of improving the constitutions,preventing the disease and promoting health by changing the convertible factors.

title: Distribution Rule of TCM Constitution and Relevant Factors in Metabolic Syndrome
abstract: Objective To explore the distribution rule of TCM constitution in the patients of meta-bolic syndrome and the correlativity with the metabolic components. Methods TCM constitutions of 106 pa-tients of metabolic syndrome and relevant biological indicators were included in the clinical investigation. X2 test and Logistic regression were used to analyze the constituent ratio and relevant factors. Results Of 106 patients of metabolic syndrome,the proportion of neutral constitution was the highest(24 cases,22. 6% ), phlegm damp constitution and qi deficiency constitution accounted for nearly 1 / 3 of the abnormal constitu-tions. TCM constitution distribution was not different significantly between males and females and among the patients at different ages( P = 0. 145,P = 0. 934). Phlegm damp constitution was relevant with TG( P =0. 048,OR = 0. 088,95% CI:0. 008 to 0. 975). Damp heat constitution was relevant with the waistline(P =0. 047,OR = 1. 106,95% CI:1. 001 to 1. 222). Qi stagnation constitution was relevant with blood pressure(P= 0. 022,OR = 8. 392,95% CI:1. 355 to 51. 962). Conclusion TCM constitutions of patients of metabolic syndrome are regularly distributed. The individual constitution is relevant with metabolic components.

title: Study on the Relationships between TCM Constitution Types and Related Metabolic Indexes of Patients with Metabolic Syndrome
abstract: Objective:To study the relationships between TCM constitution types and related metabolic indexes of patients with metabolic syndrome,in order to provide an objective basis for TCM constitution interventions,improvement of biased constitution,and recovery of the health of individuals.Methods:By means of a cross-sectional study to collect of data and indexes of patients with metabolic syndrome;in accordance with "TCM Constitution Scale" to judge the constitution types and observe their distributions;by descriptive analysis,Logistic regression analysis etc.statistical methods,to analyze the relationships between the distribution law of TCM constitution and its related metabolic indexes.Results:Biased constitutions were in majority,the order of biased constitutions were respectively complex constitution,phlegmdamp,damp-heat,blood-stasis,qi-deficiency,yang- insufficiency,qi-stagnation,yin-deficiency,special innate.qi-stagnation,yin-deficiency,special innate.Among them,in complex constitution,phlegm-damp with blood-stasis was taken as main.The results of Logistic regression analysis showed that diastolic blood pressure is a protective factor of moderate constitution,BMI is a risk factor of phlegm-damp constitution,HDL is the protective factor of phlegm-damp constitution,diastolic blood pressure is a risk factor of damp-heat constitution,systolic blood pressure and LDL are risk factors of blood-stasis constitution,BMI and diastolic blood pressure are protective factors of blood-stasis constitution.Conclusions:phlegm-damp,damp-heat,blood-stasis are main biased constitutions of patients with metabolic syndrome;to prompt:HDL,LDL,BMI,diastolic blood pressure,systolic blood pressure etc.indexes are closely related to the improvement of constitution of patients with MS and the recovery of healthful physiological state.

title: Constitution Factors for Metabolic Syndrome: A Case-control Study
abstract: Objective To investigate the constitution factors for metabolic syndrome(MS). Methods 271 MS cases and 271 non-MS controls were selected. They were assessed according to the diagnosis standard of constitution of TCM. Results Multiple logistic regression analysis revealed that phlegm-dampness constitution and dampness-heat constitution were dangerous factors for MS, normal constitution was protective factor for MS (P0.05). Conclusion Research on constitution play a role in investigating pathogenesis of MS.

title: Relationship Between Phlegm-Dampness and Metabolic Syndrome in Aged People
abstract: Objective To explore the relationship between phlegm-dampness type and metabolic syndrome in aged people. Methods A cross-sectiona1 survey was conducted in aged people. The data was analysed by using Epidata 3. 0 database. Results 35. 3 % of all cases had MS,53. 5 % of all cases had phlegm-dampness type,35. 5% of all phlegm-dampness type had MS. In the all cases of phlegm-dampness type,49. 9% were obesity or over-weight,32. 3% had hyperglycaemia,66. 7% had hypertension,and 39. 4% had hyperlipemia. There was no obvious difference among three age-groups ( P 0. 05). Conclusion Phlegm-dampness type is related to metabolic syndrome in aged people,which provide some supports to prevent and treat us.

title: Genome-Wide DNA Methylation Profiles of Phlegm-Dampness Constitution
abstract: Background/Aims: Metabolic diseases are leading health concerns in today’s global society. In traditional Chinese medicine (TCM), one body type studied is the phlegm-dampness constitution (PC), which predisposes individuals to complex metabolic disorders. Genomic studies have revealed the potential metabolic disorders and the molecular features of PC. The role of epigenetics in the regulation of PC, however, is unknown. Methods: We analyzed a genome-wide DNA methylation in 12 volunteers using Illumina Infinium Human Methylation450 BeadChip on peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs). Eight volunteers had PC and 4 had balanced constitutions. Results: Methylation data indicated a genome-scale hyper-methylation pattern in PC. We located 288 differentially methylated probes (DMPs). A total of 256 genes were mapped, and some of these were metabolic-related. SQSTM1, DLGAP2 and DAB1 indicated diabetes mellitus; HOXC4 and SMPD3, obesity; and GRWD1 and ATP10A, insulin resistance. According to Ingenuity Pathway Analysis (IPA), differentially methylated genes were abundant in multiple metabolic pathways. Conclusion: Our results suggest the potential risk for metabolic disorders in individuals with PC. We also explain the clinical characteristics of PC with DNA methylation features.

title: Investigation and Analysis on Metabolic Syndrome and Body Constitution Identification in Teachers
abstract: Objective To investigate metabolic syndrome( MS)and TCM body constitution distribu-tion in teachers so as to better provide the scientific evidences for TCM intervention and health guidance. Methods With the participant's voluntary and the informed consent,476 teachers with annual physical ex-amination were randomized for the survey of MS and body constitution identification. Results Of 476 cases, 104 cases were MS,accounting for 21. 85﹪. For TCM constitution identification,40 cases were of neural con-stitution,accounting for 8. 4﹪;436 cases of abnormal constitution,accounting fro 91. 6﹪. Regarding TCM constitution distribution of MS,the first four top lists were 25﹪ of the cases for damp heat constitution, 19. 25﹪ of the cases for phlegm heat constitution,14. 42﹪ of the cases for yang deficiency constitution and 13. 46﹪ of the cases for qi deficiency constitution. The results were different significantly in comparison of TCM constitution of the participants without MS(χ2 =45. 13,γ= 8,P﹤0. 005). Conclusion The study provides the theoretic evidences for the interventions of MS with integrated Chinese and western medicine so as to reduce MS incidence in the teachers and improves their health. Through the identification of TCM con-stitution among specific group,the health construction for TCM prevention of disease is promoted so that TCM health management system can be accomplished gradually and the health of mankind is improved.

title: Metabolism and characteristics of acupoint measurement by conductance method among damp heat and phlegm humid people
abstract: Objective To investigate the metabolism and the characteristics of acupoint measurement by conduct -ance method among damp heat and phlegm humid people.Methods Using the method of epidemiological survey,TCM Constitution identification,blood metabolism index test and acupoint measurement using conductance method were conduc -ted in among the residents of a village of Nanhai District,Foshan.The correlation analysis was conducted using SPSS 18.0 statistic software.Results A total of 701 residents were investigated,among whom 627(92.30%)were with gentleness type,39(5.56%)with humid heat type and 35(2.14%)with phlegm and humid quality.Among them,cholesterol in-crease was revealed in 429 residents(61.20%),three acylglycerol increase in 200 residents(28.53%),blood uric acid increase in 249 residents(35.52%), and fasting blood glucose increase in 55 residents(7.85%).According to con-ductance method measurement,the most prominent deficiency type was in Sanjiao(166,23.7%); the most prominent empirical type was heat meridian(186,26.5%).Phlegm dampness was closely correlated with the increases of total cho-lesterol,high blood uric acid and fasting blood glucose;and damp heat and gentleness types were closely correlated with the increase of three acyl glycerin.Phlegm dampness was correlated with deficiency in spleen,stomach,lung,kidney and bladder meridians.Damp heat was correlated with the empirical in pericardium,small intestine,bladder,spleen meridian and gallbladder.Conclusion Damp heat and phlegm are differed in both metabolic parameters and actual measurement point conductivity distribution, it provides a reference for research, intervention and identification of diseases related to these two kinds of constitution.

title: Phlegm-Dampness Constitution: Genomics, Susceptibility, Adjustment and Treatment with Traditional Chinese Medicine
abstract: The constitution of traditional Chinese medicine was established in 1970s by Chinese scholars, in which the constitutions of Chinese people were classified into nine types for study. The phlegm-dampness constitution is one of the nine constitutions and is the most common type in constitution study. Genomics studies found four upregulated genes: COPS8, GNPDA1, CD52 and ARPC3; and six downregulated genes: GSPT2, CACNB2, FLJ20584, UXS1, IL21R and TNPO in the phlegm-dampness constitution. Gene functional analyses on genes affecting the differences between the phlegm-dampness constitution and the balanced constitution indicated that people with phlegm-dampness constitution were susceptible to hyperlipemia and diabetes. Results of epidemiological surveys also revealed that people with phlegm-dampness constitution have a much higher risk of obesity, metabolic syndrome, hypertension and diabetes than people with a balanced constitution. Therefore, differentiation of phlegm-dampness constitution could be performed in the normal population with the Constitution of Chinese Medicine Scale to estimate the risks of those diseases for prediction. For people with phlegm-dampness constitution, Chinese medicine could be used to reduce risk of related diseases. Constitution-based strategies in disease prevention and treatment are consistent with the current proposed 4P medical mode (personalized, predictive, preventive and participatory). With the rising burden of global disease and increasing medical expenditure, the objectives of medicine are transforming from treatment to prevention. Thus, studies on the phlegm-dampness constitution of traditional Chinese medicine are significantly important for the prediction and prevention of related diseases and maintenance of human health.

title: Chromatography Mass Spectrometry-Based Metabolomics Approach to Study Phlegm-Dampness Constitution of Traditional Chinese Medicine
abstract: Objective:In order to find out the symbolic serum metabolites and changes in metabolic pathways of Phlegm -dampness constitution,combing glucose and lipid metabolism,homeostasis model assessment insulin resistance to explore formation,development and pathological characteristics of phlegm-dampness constitution and pathogenesis of "obesity was prone to phlegm-dampness constitution".Methods:Collect normal control group peace constitution,overweight group non-phlegm-dampness and phlegm-dampness constitution,obesity group phlegm-dampness constitution.Compare body fat parameter[Body Mass Index (BMI),waist circumference (WC),waist-to-hipratio (WHR)],blood lipid (TG,CHOL,HDL-C,LDL-C),fasting plasma glucose(FPG),fasting serum insulin(FINS) and homeostasis model assessment insulin resistance(Homa-IR) among 4 groups and make correlation with phlegm-dampness scores.Based on HPLC-Q-TOF/MS,use multivariate statistical analysis to find out the difference serum metabolites of phlegm-dampness constitution and explore internal pathological features of phlegm-dampness constitution and pathogenesis of related -disease.Results:BMI,WC,WHR and TG increased in turn,while HDL-C decreased.FINS and Homa-IR in obesity group (phlegm-dampness constitution) was higher than that of normal control group (peace constitution) (P ＜ 0.01).Phlegm-dampness integral had a positive correlation to BMI,WC,WHR and TG(P ＜ 0.05),genetic correlation to HDL -C(P ＜ 0.01).Eight differentially expressed metabolites in overweight group (non-phlegm-dampness constitution) can be identified.The overweight and obesity group(phlegm-dampness constitution) had 9 in each(P ＜ 0.05).Conclusion:Phlegm-dampness constitution of obesity has obvious pathological characteristics in abnormal body fat distribution,lipid metabolism disorders,high-FINS level and insulin resistance.Phlegm-dampness is the susceptible constitution of obesity and it has different serum metabolites.
